steps.title: Identify the grammatical structures around the blank
steps.content: On the left: an independent clause ending with “three primary...” (needing a noun). On the right: a list of three items, not a clause.

steps.title: Apply the rule for lists
steps.content:
A colon introduces a list or elaboration after an independent clause.

Choice C uses a colon to properly introduce the list of three periods that elaborate on “three primary periods.” The other three either create a sentence fragment with no verb (A), misuse a semicolon for what are not two independent clauses (B), or use a comma instead of the required colon (D).

That matches choice C — C is correct.
